title: Daughter Finds Mother's Decapitated Body In Backyard

beats.description: Cold open establishes the crime and daughter's suspicion before flashing back to the beginning.
Initial police welfare check where Martha's erratic behavior is dismissed and David waits inside.
Narrator details the brutal murder, concealment, and David's false sense of security.
Kim reports her mother missing, but police advise against filing a formal report due to mental health history.
Kim investigates independently, police conduct a second flawed welfare check, and the case goes cold.
Kim receives bizarre texts from Martha's phone, realizing David is impersonating her.
Police break into the empty house, find suspicious absences, and set up surveillance after a neighbor tip.
Police confront and arrest David on unrelated warrants, but lack concrete evidence for the murder.
Anonymous tip directs family to the backyard, prompting a forensic search and neighbor's fire/smell testimony.
Body is discovered, detectives confront David with the evidence, and he breaks down into a full confession.

_analysis_metadata.transcript_preview: Aer, please just come outside. How you doing? Is Martha here? >> No, she's probably in her boyfriend's house. >> 30 days ago, 53-year-old David Callahan killed his girlfriend Martha and buried her body in his own backyard. David thinks he's gotten away with his horrific crime. Martha was mentally ill, had few friends, and no one was supposed to come looking for her. Martha's daughter Kim was the only person concerned about her mother's whereabouts. >> If I come up missing or dead, it is no accident. And he has told her before, if I I'm going to get your ass and nobody's ever going to find you. >> What's your concern? >> That he killed her. ...
